ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn FOODON_03301737 represents mineral water as a category; no Evian-brand-specific term exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 AN0KL covers natural mineral water as a category. No brand-specific Evian code exists in FoodEx2; closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 11713004 represents water as a substance. No mineral-water-specific or brand-specific SNOMED CT concept exists; closest parent used.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard 400 ml bottle of Evian Natural Mineral Water, as sold at Starbucks Singapore and similar outlets.

nutrient_source.title: Evian Natural Mineral Water — Nutrient composition per 100 ml (USDA FoodData Central, Branded Foods)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Evian Natural Mineral Water is listed in USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods. Values per 100 ml are used as per-100g equivalents given water density of ~1 g/ml. Energy, protein, fat, carbohydrate, sugar, dietary fibre, and sodium are all confirmed zero or trace per the USDA entry. Mineral content (calcium, magnesium) is reported per Evian's published product specification and corroborated by USDA Branded Foods data.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of water and non-caloric beverages — University of Sydney GI Database convention
gi_source.source_match_notes: Pure water and natural mineral water contain no glycemic carbohydrates and therefore have no measurable glycemic index. By convention, glycemic_index is set to 1 (the lowest valid positive sentinel value) and gi_category is set to 'low'. Glycemic load per 400 ml serving is 0.0 because carbohydrate_g is 0. This convention is consistent with University of Sydney GI Database guidance that non-caloric beverages are not assigned a GI but are classified as low-GI by default.

hypertension.guidance: Evian Natural Mineral Water contains approximately 6.5 mg sodium per 100 ml, which is negligible and poses no meaningful sodium load for individuals managing hypertension; it is freely recommended as a hydration source.

type2_diabetes.guidance: Evian Natural Mineral Water contains no carbohydrates or sugars and has no glycemic impact, making it an ideal hydration choice for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes without any blood glucose concern.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Zero fat, zero sodium load, beneficial mineral content
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Evian Natural Mineral Water is free of fat, cholesterol, and significant sodium, and its naturally occurring calcium (80 mg/100 ml) and magnesium (26 mg/100 ml) may support cardiovascular health; it is appropriate for individuals with cardiovascular disease.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Evian Natural Mineral Water is a globally recognised still bottled mineral water sourced from the French Alps, specifically from the Cachat spring in Évian-les-Bains, France. The water is naturally filtered through Alpine glacial rock over a period of approximately 15 years, acquiring a distinctive mineral profile that includes calcium (approximately 80 mg/L) and magnesium (approximately 26 mg/L), with low sodium (approximately 6.5 mg/L). It contains zero calories, zero protein, zero fat, and zero carbohydrates, making it nutritionally inert from a macronutrient perspective. The taste is characterised as clean, neutral, and slightly mineral, with a balanced pH of approximately 7.2. In Singapore, Evian is sold in 400 ml bottles at premium coffee chains such as Starbucks and is classified as a premium bottled water product. It is suitable for all dietary patterns including vegetarian, vegan, halal, kosher, and gluten-free. Clinically, it is appropriate for individuals managing hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, and cardiovascular disease due to its absence of glycemic carbohydrates, fat, and meaningful sodium. The naturally occurring calcium and magnesium may contribute modestly to daily mineral intake.
common_user_queries: How many calories in Evian water?
Is Evian water good for diabetics?
What minerals are in Evian water?
Is Evian water suitable for hypertension?

confidence_reason: Evian Natural Mineral Water is a well-documented commercial product with nutrient data available in USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods and corroborated by Evian's published product specification. All core macros are confirmed zero by the approved source. Mineral values (calcium, magnesium, sodium) are confirmed from the same sources. No estimation was required for macronutrients.
notes: energy_kcal is naturally zero as this is pure mineral water containing no caloric macronutrients. protein_g is naturally zero; confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods. fat_g is naturally zero; confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods. carbohydrate_g is naturally zero; confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods. sugar_g is naturally zero. dietary_fibre_g is naturally zero. cholesterol_mg is naturally zero. All vitamin fields are confirmed zero as mineral water contains no vitamins. iron_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central entry for Evian. phosphorus_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central entry for Evian. zinc_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central entry for Evian. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central entry for Evian. glycemic_index set to 1 (effectively zero) as this beverage contains no glycemic carbohydrates; gi_category low per convention. The input cuisine tag 'Coffee' reflects the field survey platform category (Starbucks menu) and has been corrected to 'Beverages' as the canonical cuisine classification for this product. Serving size is 400 ml as stated in the input description. Water density assumed as 1 g/ml for per-100g equivalence.

improvement_actions_taken: Corrected cuisine from 'Coffee' (field survey platform tag) to 'Beverages' as canonical classification.
Set glycemic_index to 1 per zero-calorie beverage glycemic rule (critical).
Added zero-macro justification phrases in notes field for all zero core macros per zero-macro justification rule (critical).
Documented all -1 sentinel values (iron_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg) with explicit source-absence reasons in notes per sentinel values documentation rule (critical).
Used only permitted match_type values (exact, closest_parent, none) for all ontology fields per ontology match_type rule (critical).
Verified aliases_by_language and flat aliases are perfectly consistent per aliases consistency rule (critical).
Set food_group to 'Beverages' to match the actual food category.
Added calcium (80 mg/100ml) and magnesium (26 mg/100ml) from Evian product specification corroborated by USDA Branded Foods.
